hard.ais_why: AIS field reads Strong (4.0) but the field note flags no recent AI cue and going quiet since late February; the 5 Nov 2025 discovery call CONTRADICTS the 'no AI cue' note: Anthony Ribadeneira explicitly asked for an AI-agent-first platform, so the underlying AI appetite is real and the field's silence note reflects only the 4-month dormancy, not the buyer's stance.
hard.primary_competitor: None named (do-nothing / status quo is the live threat)

north_star_verdict.headline: Dormant since the 24 Feb 2026 last activity: the single 5 Nov 2025 discovery call showed real AI-first appetite from Anthony Ribadeneira, but there is no champion advocacy, no economic buyer engaged, and no RFQ, and Anthony never returned after his May 2026 note to reconnect once budgeting cleared. The live threat is do-nothing, not a rival.
north_star_verdict.math: Created and qualified same day, 5 November 2025. In Qualified for 243 days (8.1 months) as of 6 July 2026, far beyond the 30-60 day enterprise norm for Qualified. LastActivityDate 24 February 2026 (132 days ago); no verified buyer engagement since. Only one buyer discovery call on record (5 Nov 2025, 8 months ago); no demo, no RFQ, no second meeting. Field history shows only empty Next_Step edits (None to None). Close date 31 December 2026 (178 days out) is a placeholder; at current zero velocity the deal will not reach Formal Evaluation, let alone close, on that date.
evidence: Only one buyer call on record: discovery call 5 November 2025 (8 months ago). The 18 November 2025 'PLaN: Intake to Outcomes' entry has no transcript and reads as an internal planning call, not a buyer meeting.
LastActivityDate 24 February 2026 (132 days ago); no verified buyer engagement since.
Next Step (dated May 2026) states Anthony is working through internal budgeting and stakeholders and will reach back; that is a rep-side wait, not buyer engagement, and no contact has followed.
In Qualified for 243 days with no progression to Formal Evaluation or Shortlisted and no RFQ issued.
No economic buyer engaged, no active champion, no quantified business case, no named competitor.
Amount $100k on a deal Edward himself sized at roughly $500k subscription plus 1.25x implementation on 5 Nov 2025, so the figure is a placeholder.

metrics.narrative: Anthony Ribadeneira (Head of Procurement) articulated clear qualitative pain on the 5 November 2025 call but no quantified value case exists. Progyny runs procurement entirely manually ('everything is being done in emails, Excels, PDFs') with a procurement function he had stood up only a month or two earlier, and he wants a platform to give visibility into how the business engages procurement company-wide. When Edward offered Zycus's pro-bono value-engineering / ROI benchmarking against Hackett and Aberdeen data, Anthony declined for now ('to the extent that would be helpful... we're just not there just yet'), preferring to first see how the interim procurement process performs company-wide. No savings target, cycle-time, headcount, or spend-under-management number was stated. The move is to co-build the ROI once the interim-process feedback exists, which is now overdue given the 8-month gap.
sources: Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Anthony Ribadeneira: 'if everything is being done in emails, Excels, PDFs, how is that kind of analysis run?'
Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Anthony Ribadeneira: 'to the extent that would be helpful... we're just not there just yet'
Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Edward Dlugosz offered pro-bono value engineering benchmarked to Hackett and Aberdeen

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named in Salesforce (the only contact role is Anthony Ribadeneira, Head of Procurement, Decision Maker) or engaged on any call. The evidence does, however, point at where budget authority sits: Anthony said on 5 November 2025 that 'my manager obviously reached out in terms of getting some insights for budgeting,' indicating a person above Anthony owns or gates the platform budget. That manager is unnamed in every source, so promoting them or attaching a title would be fabrication. Anthony also framed the spend himself, so he has strong influence but not confirmed final budget sign-off. The move is to ask Anthony to introduce the budget-owning manager and map the real economic buyer before the RFQ is scoped.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: H. Anthony Ribadeneira (Head of Procurement, Decision Maker, primary)
Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Anthony Ribadeneira: 'my manager obviously reached out in terms of getting some insights for budgeting'

decision_criteria.narrative: Anthony named a clear leading criterion on 5 November 2025: a genuinely AI-agent-first platform, and he was explicit it would be decisive ('that would be probably a priority for the vendors that I shortlist'). He wants an agent that answers routine buyer questions autonomously (his ChatGPT / car-buying analogy) yet remains showtime-ready today, having found rival vendors' AI immature ('maybe in two or 3 years, but not now'). Secondary criteria surfaced: AI data privacy (whether Merlin data is public or ring-fenced), sourcing plus procurement orchestration scope (not full source-to-pay), and integration with their iRequest system. The formal criteria will be shaped by the interim procurement process document Progyny is publishing, which Anthony said would 'inform... what the platform capability should be.' Criteria are emerging and buyer-stated, but not yet formalized in an RFQ, so partial.
sources: Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Anthony Ribadeneira: 'I would like to take an AI agent first approach if possible'
Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Anthony Ribadeneira: 'None seem to me like they really have an AI first solution... maybe in two or 3 years, but not now'
Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Anthony Ribadeneira: 'we'll probably use iRequest... plug into iRequest'

decision_process.narrative: Anthony described the process he intends to run on 5 November 2025: publish an interim company-wide procurement process 'relatively soon', let the organization adopt it and gather feedback, then use that document to scope the platform, then run an RFQ for the platform. Legal governs the timing: NDAs are issued only after vendors are shortlisted, not before the RFQ. He originally floated getting a platform live 'early in the New Year' with negotiations and contracting before that, but that timeline has lapsed with no RFQ. Budget insights are being gathered via his manager. The process is buyer-owned and single-threaded through Anthony today; no procurement/IT/legal stakeholders have been engaged by Zycus. Partial: the shape is known but no RFQ date or committee has materialized.
sources: Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Anthony Ribadeneira: 'that procurement process should be published relatively soon... use that document as a guide to potentially inform the scoping out of a platform'
Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Anthony Ribadeneira: 'they'd rather put NDAs in place once vendors are shortlisted'
Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Anthony Ribadeneira: 'getting a platform up and running early in the New Year'

paper_process.narrative: Two contracting mechanics are known from 5 November 2025 and both are friction points. First, Progyny's legal team prefers to issue NDAs only after shortlisting, while Zycus does not release pricing without an NDA; Edward flagged this as 'a small issue for us', and Anthony offered to try to arrange a time-bound, narrow-scope NDA to cover the RFQ. Second, the platform must integrate with Progyny's iRequest system, which Anthony intends to keep for contracting/requisitioning, so an adapter is in scope. Implementation was framed as roughly 1.25x the software cost as a rule of thumb, to be refined into a fixed-bid SOW after a scoping session. No InfoSec review, MSA/SOW drafting, or procurement channel has been discussed, and no scoping session has occurred. Partial: mechanics are outlined but nothing is in motion.
sources: Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Anthony Ribadeneira: 'they'd rather put NDAs in place once vendors are shortlisted'
Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Edward Dlugosz: 'We don't normally release pricing... not under an NDA. That might be a small issue for us'
Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Edward Dlugosz: implementation about 1.25x software, refined into a fixed-bid SOW after a scoping session

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is confirmed and owned by Anthony Ribadeneira (Head of Procurement). Progyny's procurement function is newly established (Anthony had been in seat under two months when they first met) and runs with no platform, entirely on emails, Excel, and PDFs. That creates three concrete pains he articulated: no visibility into how the business engages procurement company-wide ('how are we being engaged? what are the needs?'), no way to measure adoption or cycle times of the interim process he is rolling out, and no system to guide or automate routine buyer requests, forcing his small team to field everything manually. The urgency is real enough that he was exploring a platform within his first two months, but he wants the interim process feedback first before committing. Pain owner: Anthony Ribadeneira.
sources: Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Anthony Ribadeneira: 'if everything is being done in emails, Excels, PDFs, how is that kind of analysis run?'
Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Anthony Ribadeneira: 'let's see how this procurement process goes just company wide. How are we being engaged? What are the needs?'
Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025, Anthony Ribadeneira: 'now we're kind of starting to turn our attention towards a potential platform'

champion.narrative: There is no active champion. Anthony Ribadeneira is the Decision Maker contact role and was receptive and engaged on the 5 November 2025 call, asking detailed questions about Merlin AI, pricing, and data privacy, but engagement stopped there: 8 months on there has been no follow-up from him, no introduction to his budget-owning manager, IT, or legal, and no evidence he has advocated Zycus internally. He explicitly deferred the ROI and asked Zycus to wait ('feel free to ping me... in a month'), placing us in a passive wait state that has now run four months past his May 2026 note to reconnect. He is a friendly, senior contact, not a coach or advocate. What is missing: any internal selling, multi-threading, or forward pull. The move is to re-engage him with a differentiated AI proof point and convert interest into advocacy plus a stakeholder introduction.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: H. Anthony Ribadeneira (Head of Procurement, Decision Maker, primary)
Avoma discovery call 5 November 2025: Anthony engaged and receptive but deferred next steps ('ping me... in a month')

Next Step (May 2026): 'Anthony is working through... internal budgeting and stakeholders... reach back to you once he has it' — passive, no advocacy; LastActivityDate 24 February 2026, no multi-threading since

competition.narrative: No competitor is named in Salesforce or on any call. Anthony said on 5 November 2025 that he had 'spoken to many vendors in the space' but that 'none seem to me like they really have an AI first solution' and that their AI is 'maybe in two or 3 years, but not now' — so a quiet multi-vendor scan is implied, but he named no one, and inventing rivals would be fabrication. The real current threat is not a vendor: it is do-nothing / status quo. Progyny is still standing up its interim procurement process and Anthony has repeatedly gated a platform decision on seeing how that beds in, so the most likely outcome absent re-engagement is that Progyny keeps running procurement manually and defers the buy. The wedge, if re-engaged, is to make Merlin Intake and ANA the showtime-ready agent Anthony said the market lacks, backed by the PWC reference (which replaced two incumbents with Zycus for AI), before any RFQ locks requirements.

The single strongest current threat is therefore do-nothing / status quo: Progyny is a newly-built procurement function still running on emails, Excel, and PDFs, and Anthony has repeatedly gated a platform decision on first seeing how the interim process performs company-wide, so the likeliest outcome absent re-engagement is a no-decision, not a loss to a rival. The wedge is Merlin Intake and ANA as the showtime-ready AI agents Anthony said the market lacks.

best_practice_check.summary: This is an early, single-threaded, dormant deal with a real AI-first wedge that is going unused. The highest-impact levers are to re-open the relationship, multi-thread to the budget owner, and land the differentiated AI proof before Progyny scopes its RFQ without us.
flags: Re-open now: Anthony has been silent 132 days; a value-led AI-first touch is the single highest-impact move before the interim-process RFQ is scoped without Zycus.
Multi-thread beyond Anthony: map his budget-owning manager and the iRequest/IT owner so the deal survives if the one thread stays quiet.
Weaponize the AI wedge: get Merlin Intake and ANA in front of Anthony as the showtime-ready agent he said rivals lack, with the PWC reference, to differentiate before requirements lock.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A newly-built procurement function that liked our AI-first story on the single 5 Nov 2025 discovery call, then went dark for four-plus months; there is no champion advocacy, no economic buyer, no business case, and no RFQ, and the live threat is Progyny deciding to keep buying manually rather than committing now.

win_position.text: We are one unqualified discovery call in: Anthony Ribadeneira (Head of Procurement) liked the Merlin AI-first pitch on 5 Nov 2025, but with no economic buyer engaged, no champion advocacy, and no RFQ, this is an early, unproven opportunity.
The deal has been dark since the 24 Feb 2026 last activity and Anthony never returned despite his May 2026 note to reach back after internal budgeting, so re-engagement is unproven.
No rival is named, but do-nothing is the real threat: Progyny is still standing up its interim manual process and may keep running on emails, Excel, and PDFs rather than buy now (Avoma, 5 Nov 2025).

One genuine wedge remains: Anthony explicitly wants an AI-agent-first platform and said rival vendors' AI is 'maybe two or three years' away, matching Merlin Intake and ANA plus the PWC AI reference (Avoma, 5 Nov 2025).

deal_momentum.text: No forward movement in 243 days in Qualified: one discovery call on 5 Nov 2025, no demo, no RFQ, no second meeting, and field history shows only empty Next_Step edits.
The only planned next step is passive: the May 2026 note has the rep waiting for Anthony to reach back once he has clarity, with no customer-set date.

customer_commitment.text: Anthony declined the pro-bono ROI / value-engineering offer ('we're just not there just yet'), so no business case exists (Avoma, 5 Nov 2025).
The deal is single-threaded on one contact who has gone silent for over four months and has not multi-threaded Zycus to any other stakeholder.

deal_risk.text: Do-nothing / status-quo is the primary risk: Progyny may keep managing procurement manually while the new function matures, a no-decision rather than a competitive loss.
Zycus's no-pricing-without-NDA stance clashes with Progyny legal's NDA-after-shortlist policy, a friction flagged on 5 Nov 2025 that could exclude us from the RFQ.
The economic buyer is unmapped: budget sits with Anthony's unnamed manager, who has never been met (Avoma, 5 Nov 2025).
